content.h1: Free Professional Invoice Generator 2026 – Compliant with Article 293 B & Reverse Charge Rules

h2_sections.intro: Complete invoicing solution compliant with 2026 tax requirements
h2_sections.article293b: Article 293 B: VAT exemption scheme explained
h2_sections.autoliquidation: VAT Reverse Charge: Mechanism and application
h2_sections.mentions_legales: Mandatory legal information on your invoices
h2_sections.avantages: Why choose InvoiceGEN for your invoicing?

content.intro_paragraph: InvoiceGEN is the reference tool for easily creating invoices compliant with French and European tax legislation. Our solution integrates all mandatory information: unique sequential invoice number, issue date, company name, address, SIRET or SIREN number, NAF/APE code, intra-community VAT number (if applicable), as well as advanced options such as VAT reverse charge. Thanks to a no-signup and completely free interface, freelancers, craftsmen, micro-entrepreneurs, and SMEs generate legally valid documents in seconds, directly from their browser, with no installation or complex configuration. Our generator strictly complies with the French General Tax Code and anticipates the e-invoicing reform coming into effect in 2026. Whether you are subject to VAT or benefit from the VAT exemption scheme, our tool automatically adapts to your tax situation to generate perfectly compliant invoices.
content.article293b_full: Article 293 B of the French General Tax Code concerns businesses benefiting from the VAT exemption scheme. This simplified regime applies to companies whose annual turnover does not exceed certain thresholds: €91,900 for services and liberal professions, and €94,300 for trading and accommodation activities (2026 thresholds). Businesses under this regime do not charge VAT to their customers and cannot reclaim it on their purchases. Our tool automatically adjusts the wording "VAT not applicable, Article 293 B of the French Tax Code" to avoid any tax errors. This legal mention is imperative to justify the absence of VAT on the invoice and to protect against potential tax audits. For VAT-liable businesses, the reverse charge module handles intra-community service provisions and subcontracting work in the construction sector. The VAT exemption scheme considerably simplifies accounting management for small structures, allowing them to focus on their core business without the administrative complexity associated with VAT declaration and payment.
content.autoliquidation_full: VAT reverse charge is a tax mechanism that transfers the obligation to declare and pay VAT from the service provider to the customer. This mechanism, provided for in Article 283-2 of the French Tax Code and European Directive 2006/112/EC, mainly concerns: 1) Service provisions between taxable persons established in different EU member states; 2) Operations in the construction sector; 3) Deliveries of certain electronic products, telecommunications, and broadcasting services; 4) Transfers of greenhouse gas emission quotas. Our generator offers dedicated fields for reverse charge, allowing you to indicate "VAT reverse charged by the customer" or "Reverse charge - Article 283". You can also configure multiple VAT rates: standard rate (20%), intermediate rate (10% for restaurants, transport, etc.), reduced rate (5.5% for energy, books, etc.) or super-reduced rate (2.1% for press, reimbursable medicines).
content.mentions_legales_full: In accordance with Articles L. 441-3 and L. 441-4 of the French Commercial Code, as well as provisions of the General Tax Code, any professional invoice must contain mandatory information subject to nullity and tax penalties. InvoiceGEN automatically includes: unique sequential invoice number; invoice issue date; delivery or service date if different; name, company name, address, and SIRET number of the seller; name, address of the customer (if professional, their SIRET and intra-community VAT number); NAF/APE code of the company; detailed description of products or services, with quantity and unit price excluding VAT; VAT rate and amount applicable per product or service; payment terms (deadlines, discounts for early payment, late payment penalties); late payment penalty rate (legal interest rate + 10 points); fixed recovery indemnity amount (€40); specific tax regime mentions: "VAT not applicable, Article 293 B" for exemptions, or "VAT reverse charged" for reverse charges.
content.tva_intra_full: For cross-border transactions, intra-community VAT management is crucial. Our tool handles goods deliveries and service provisions between EU member states, as well as exports outside the EU. You can enter your intra-community VAT number (FR + key + SIREN) and that of your customer, and the generator automatically applies territoriality rules. Goods deliveries between member states are VAT-exempt subject to proof of transport and possession of a valid VAT number (Article 262 ter). Intra-community acquisitions are taxed in the customer's country. For service provisions, the place of taxation depends on the customer's status: if the customer is a taxable person (professional), the service is taxed in their country (reverse charge); if the customer is a private individual, it is taxed in the provider's country.
content.reforme_2026_full: The e-invoicing reform comes into force progressively from 2026. From September 1, 2026, all business-to-business transactions (B2B) involving VAT-liable entities must be transmitted via a partner dematerialization platform or via the public invoicing portal. InvoiceGEN anticipates this reform by structuring your data according to e-invoice standards: UBL (Universal Business Language) and CII (Cross Industry Invoice) formats. Our tool generates invoices in PDF/A-3 format, a legal archiving format recognized by tax authorities, compatible with future dematerialized transmission obligations. Mandatory data for e-invoicing includes: unique invoice identifier, issue date, nature of the operation, customer VAT code, payment method, and certification body mentions.
content.avantages_full: Unlike many invoicing software programs that impose monthly subscriptions (€10 to €50/month) or invoice limitations, InvoiceGEN remains 100% free with no signup required. No account creation is needed: you instantly access the tool and generate as many invoices as necessary, with no commitment or limit. Your data remains on your device (localStorage) and is never stored on our servers, ensuring absolute confidentiality and security. The interface has been designed to be intuitive and accessible on all devices: computer, tablet, smartphone. The local storage system allows you to find your in-progress invoices even after closing your browser. Our exclusive advantages: professional PDF export with customizable design; automatic calculation of totals (excluding VAT, VAT, including VAT); management of discounts, deposits, credit notes; compliance with NF EN 16931 standard (e-invoicing); permanent updates according to tax developments; multilingual support French/English; RGAA-compliant accessibility.
content.bonnes_pratiques_full: For impeccable professional invoicing, here are our expert tips: 1) Establish uninterrupted chronological numbering (e.g., INV2026-001) to facilitate accounting tracking and tax audits. 2) Always mention payment deadlines (30 days end of month, 60 days, etc.) and late payment penalty rates (3 times the minimum legal interest rate). 3) For self-employed individuals, specify the micro-social regime and the optional lump-sum payment if applicable. 4) Keep your invoices for at least 10 years as required by the Commercial Code. 5) For international service provisions, systematically verify the validity of VAT numbers via VIES. 6) For deposits, issue a deposit invoice followed by a final invoice settling the service. 7) Use secure payment methods and trace all transactions. 8) For construction works subject to reverse charge, explicitly mention the reason for reverse charge. 9) Comply with GDPR: only collect necessary data and inform your customers. 10) Automate reminders for unpaid invoices.
content.cta: Start creating your 2026-compliant invoice now – 100% free, no signup, no commitment

blogPost2026.title: 2026 Invoicing Reform: The Complete Guide for Freelancers & SMEs
blogPost2026.description: Everything freelancers and small businesses need to know about mandatory electronic invoicing in France starting in 2026.
blogPost2026.author: InvoiceGEN Team
blogPost2026.intro: September 1, 2026, will mark a historic turning point for the French economy. Electronic invoicing will no longer be an option, but the legal standard for all companies, from micro-entrepreneurs to large groups.
blogPost2026.section1Content: The reform is not just a simple digitization; it's a deep transformation of financial flows. Here are the three pillars to remember:
blogPost2026.change1: Reception Obligation: From September 1, 2026, you must be able to receive structured electronic invoices from your suppliers (SMEs and Large Companies).
blogPost2026.change2: E-invoicing: Sending invoices will no longer be done by simple email, but via certified platforms (PDP) or the public portal (PPF) to guarantee integrity.
blogPost2026.change3: E-reporting: For your sales to individuals (B2C) or international transactions, data must be transmitted automatically to the tax administration.
blogPost2026.section2Title: Checklist: The 5 Mandatory Mentions to be Compliant
blogPost2026.section2Content: To avoid automatic rejections by your clients' systems and fines from the DGFiP, check these 5 points:
blogPost2026.mention1: Client's SIREN Number: Mandatory for unique identification in the central directory.
blogPost2026.mention2: Delivery Address: If it differs from the head office, it must appear clearly.
blogPost2026.mention3: Type of Operation: Specify if it's a delivery of goods, services, or mixed.
blogPost2026.mention4: Option for Debit: If you pay VAT on debits, the mention is now required.
blogPost2026.mention5: Original Invoice Number: Essential for any credit note or corrective invoice.
blogPost2026.section3Title: Why a Simple PDF Will No Longer Suffice (Factur-X / ZUGFeRD)
blogPost2026.section3Content: A classic PDF is a human-readable image, but 'blind' for a machine. The reform imposes the Factur-X format.
blogPost2026.facturXDesc: Factur-X is a hybrid file: a visual PDF for you, and an embedded invisible XML file for automatic processing. This is what's called a structured invoice.
blogPost2026.section4Title: How InvoiceGEN Automates This Compliance for Free
blogPost2026.section4Content: We designed InvoiceGEN so you don't have to become a tax law expert. Our tool takes care of everything in the background.
blogPost2026.feature1: Smart Fields: New mandatory mentions are integrated by default.
blogPost2026.feature2: PDF/A Export: Your documents comply with long-term legal archiving standards.
blogPost2026.feature3: Local-First: Total privacy, your data never leaves your computer.
blogPost2026.faqTitle: Frequently Asked Questions About the Reform
blogPost2026.q1: Am I concerned if I am exempt from VAT?
blogPost2026.a1: Yes. Even if you don't collect VAT, you must issue electronic invoices and be able to receive them if you work with taxable companies.
blogPost2026.q2: What is the risk in case of non-compliance?
blogPost2026.a2: A fine of €15 per missing mention and especially the risk that your client cannot technically pay you via their automated system.
blogPost2026.q3: What is Article 293 B of the CGI?
blogPost2026.a3: Article 293 B defines the VAT exemption scheme for businesses with annual turnover below €91,900 (services) or €94,300 (commerce). The mandatory mention 'VAT not applicable, Article 293 B' must appear on invoices.
blogPost2026.a4: Reverse charge (Article 283-2) transfers VAT obligation from provider to customer. The mention 'VAT reverse charged - Article 283' applies to intra-community services and construction works.

faq.heroSubtitle: Everything you need to know about modern billing, Article 293 B, reverse charge, and 2026 compliance.
faq.q1: What is Article 293 B of the CGI and how to apply it on my invoices?
faq.a1: Article 293 B of the French General Tax Code defines the VAT exemption scheme. This regime applies to companies whose annual turnover does not exceed €91,900 for services and €94,300 for trading activities (2026 thresholds). Companies under this regime do not charge VAT to their customers. The mandatory mention 'VAT not applicable, Article 293 B of the French Tax Code' must appear on each invoice. InvoiceGEN automatically adds this mention when you select the exemption scheme.
faq.q2: How does VAT reverse charge work?
faq.a2: VAT reverse charge is a mechanism provided for in Article 283-2 that transfers VAT obligation from the provider to the customer. This mechanism mainly concerns service provisions between taxable persons in different EU member states, operations in the construction sector, and deliveries of electronic products. The mention 'VAT reverse charged by the customer - Article 283' must appear on the invoice.
faq.q3: How does InvoiceGEN guarantee compliance with the 2026 electronic invoicing reform?
faq.a3: InvoiceGEN natively integrates all mandatory mentions required by the new French regulations. Our documents are generated in high-definition PDF/A-3 format, optimized for legal archiving. We closely follow the developments of UBL and CII formats to ensure that your exports will remain compatible with partner dematerialization platforms (PDP) and the public billing portal (PPF) as soon as they are fully deployed in 2026.
faq.q4: Are my bank and client data stored on your servers?
faq.a4: Absolutely not. InvoiceGEN is based on a 'Local-First' architecture. All the information you enter (client details, bank details, amounts) remains exclusively in your browser's local memory. Nothing is sent, stored, or processed on our servers. This is the technical guarantee of absolute confidentiality: you are the sole owner of your data.
faq.q5: Why is this service free and without registration?
faq.a5: Our mission is to remove administrative barriers for freelancers. By using your device's local storage, we eliminate the massive costs associated with hosting cloud databases. This technical efficiency allows us to offer a high-end professional tool for free, without needing to sell your data or impose a subscription on you.
